correctness.notes: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1, \label{thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed}: The reproduced identified-set argument has missing sharpness residuals/selectors: to conclude Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] one needs feasible measurable selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U, or epsilon-selectors with residuals r_L=integral{int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon-L_x(P)} dP_X -> 0 and r_U=integral{U_x(P)-int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon} dP_X -> 0, plus an interpolation argument. The theorem proves only pointwise no-gap dual endpoint values L_x(P)=D_x(h_x), U_x(P)=-D_x(-h_x), and explicitly disclaims endpoint attainment, measurable argmax selectors, and Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)].

perItemFindings.label: k-sharp-dual-confirmed
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1, \label{thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed}: The reproduced identified-set argument has missing sharpness residuals/selectors: to conclude Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] one needs feasible measurable selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U, or epsilon-selectors with residuals r_L=integral{int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon-L_x(P)} dP_X -> 0 and r_U=integral{U_x(P)-int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon} dP_X -> 0, plus an interpolation argument. The theorem proves only pointwise no-gap dual endpoint values L_x(P)=D_x(h_x), U_x(P)=-D_x(-h_x), and explicitly disclaims endpoint attainment, measurable argmax selectors, and Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)]. → The original sharp identified interval claim in Conjecture 1 is not confirmed; the result establishes only an outer-envelope dual representation, not sharpness or closed endpoint attainability.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[blocking, conjecture:k-sharp-dual-confirmed] Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1, \label{thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed}: The reproduced identified-set argument has missing sharpness residuals/selectors: to conclude Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] one needs feasible measurable selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U, or epsilon-selectors with residuals r_L=integral{int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon-L_x(P)} dP_X -> 0 and r_U=integral{U_x(P)-int h_x dgamma_x^epsilon} dP_X -> 0, plus an interpolation argument. The theorem proves only pointwise no-gap dual endpoint values L_x(P)=D_x(h_x), U_x(P)=-D_x(-h_x), and explicitly disclaims endpoint attainment, measurable argmax selectors, and Theta_I(P)=[L_rho(P),U_rho(P)].. Fix: The original sharp identified interval claim in Conjecture 1 is not confirmed; the result establishes only an outer-envelope dual representation, not sharpness or closed endpoint attainability.

Referee notes: I reproduced the Spearman-band dual signs, the endpoint/identified-set implications, and the sample/bootstrap endpoint expansions. The dual and expansion algebra match, but the sharp-dual target drops the actual sharpness and selector/attainment terms required by the original Conjecture 1.
